prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of phenol performed with the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill using DFT at tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0/multiplicity 1 for the neutral species (cation: charge +1 mult 2; anion: charge −1 mult 2). Geometry taken directly from the user-supplied .xyz file (no relaxation). Partial charges computed via the Mulliken scheme (PySCF). All three SCFs converged. Integrity checks: trustworthy = true, status = ok. Fukui charge conservation: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000.

Headline result: most electrophilic site (largest f+) is atom 3 (C), f+ = 2.1028319. Most nucleophilic site (largest f−) is atom 1 (O), f− = 0.16201477.

No engine warnings were reported. The result is consistent with phenol's known ortho/para-directing character and the nucleophilicity of the hydroxyl oxygen.
